# LibraVDB

> Local-first, three-tier memory engine for the OpenClaw AI assistant.

LibraVDB gives AI persistent, scoped memory across sessions without cloud dependencies. A Go sidecar (libravdbd) handles ONNX embedding, vector storage, and retrieval ranking. A TypeScript plugin integrates directly with OpenClaw.

## Key Facts

- Fully local: all embedding, summarization, and retrieval happen on-device
- Three-tier scoping: ephemeral, session, and long-term memory
- Hybrid retrieval: semantic (vector cosine) + keyword (BM25) ranking
- Embedding model: Nomic Embed v1.5 via ONNX Runtime (~16ms per op)
- Extractive compaction with T5 summarizer keeps memory lean
- Go sidecar communicates over local Unix socket
- Homebrew-managed daemon lifecycle on macOS
- Plugin declares kind: ["memory", "context-engine"] for dual-slot activation

## Install

```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/xDarkicex/openclaw-memory-libravdb/main/install.sh | bash
```

## Links

- Homepage: https://libravdb.com
- Documentation: https://libravdb.com/docs
- FAQ: https://libravdb.com/faq
- Benchmarks: https://libravdb.com/benchmarks

- Install Docs: https://github.com/xDarkicex/openclaw-memory-libravdb/blob/main/docs/install.md

## Pages

- /: Landing page with install command, features, and integrations overview
- /docs: Installation, architecture, configuration, and scope reference
- /faq: Frequently asked questions about LibraVDB
- /benchmarks: Performance benchmarks (embedding latency, retrieval, compaction, storage)
